campus: Genoa (Department of Economics, Via Vivaldi 5 - Darsena)
admission_type: open access (free access, but admission requires meeting curricular requirements and, where applicable, passing an assessment of adequacy of personal preparation)

academic_requirements.summary: Open-access (non-competitive) master's programme. Admission requires a recognised bachelor's/three-year degree, satisfaction of curricular requirements, and verification of the adequacy of personal preparation when the graduation mark is below the set threshold.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree, a three-year university degree, a four-year college degree, or any other degree awarded abroad and recognised as suitable.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: Either (a) a three-year degree in an economics or business class (L-33 Economics Sciences or L-18 Economics and Business Management Sciences), or (b) a three-year degree in another class together with at least 40 ECTS/CFU in the relevant scientific-disciplinary sectors covering economics (SECS-P), statistics (SECS-S), mathematics (MAT) and computer science (INF).
academic_requirements.minimum_grade: Candidates with a graduation mark of at least 99/110 (and meeting the L-33/L-18 class or sub-constraints) are exempt from the personal-preparation assessment; those with a mark below 99/110 must undergo verification. For non-EU applicants the degree grade must correspond to at least 75% of the maximum possible grade.
academic_requirements.personal_preparation_assessment: Where the threshold is not met, a written test with open questions verifies personal preparation in economics, mathematics and statistics (main topics of introductory economics courses including basic mathematics, statistics and microeconomics); candidates with significant deficiencies are deemed unsuitable but may retest.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign degree apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation, with an admission test/interview verifying both academic preparation and B2 English; the degree grade must be at least 75% of the maximum.

accepted_proofs: An official English-language certificate (e.g. IELTS, TOEFL) at B2 or higher among those accepted by the University.
A University English exam passed in Italy with a mark of at least 28/30.
Assessment via interview (reading, translating and discussing a text) if no certification is provided.
exemption_rules: Holding an accepted B2-or-higher certificate (or the qualifying CLAT certificate / 28-30 university English exam) removes the need for the English interview.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proficiency is verified at the personal-preparation assessment stage for candidates without an accepted certificate; non-EU applicants have B2 English verified during the UniGeApply pre-evaluation interview.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Assessment of Adequacy of Personal Preparation (verifica dell'adeguatezza della preparazione personale) - NOT a TOLC. A written test with open questions, plus an English interview where required.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Candidates whose graduation mark is below 99/110, or who hold a degree outside L-33/L-18 and fail the regulation sub-constraints; non-EU applicants via UniGeApply take a remote admission test/interview.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Written test with open questions verifying personal preparation in economics, mathematics and statistics; candidates needing English verification also do an interview (reading, translating and discussing a text). For non-EU pre-evaluation the test/interview is conducted remotely.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] Specific test dates are published on the Department of Economics master's admission-test calendar (https://economia.unige.it/Calendario-test-accesso-magistrali); a fixed date list was not on the course pages at the time of research.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Outcome is suitability-based: candidates with significant deficiencies are considered unsuitable (retesting permitted). Non-EU minimum degree grade is 75% of maximum.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
First-year pre-registration: from 9 April to 30 November 2026 (and until 10 March 2027 with late payment).
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment; enrolment in years after the first runs 15 July to 5 October 2026 (arrears 6 October to 5 November 2026).
application_deadline_eu.notes: Open-access (non-ranked) programme; EU/resident applicants who do not meet the 99/110 threshold must pass the personal-preparation assessment before enrolling. Tuition first installment (approx. EUR 136 stamp duty + regional tax) at enrolment; second installment by 10 December 2026; third by 23 June 2027.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.

No programme-specific early closure is published for Economics and Data Science (only Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Maritime Science and Technology, and the Computer Engineering bachelor have published fall early closures).
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published for this programme (uses the standard fall-session close of 20 March 2026).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session, apply through UniGeApply (max 2 courses; 30 EUR fixed fee for visa applicants), require a degree grade of at least 75% of maximum, and pass a remote admission test/interview. The A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has now closed.

required_documents.notes: Information on educational background, additional courses, work activities or internships.
Degree must correspond to at least 75% of the maximum grade for non-EU pre-evaluation.
List of exams taken with number of credits and grades; used to verify the 40 ECTS curricular requirement.

required_documents.document_name: English Language Proficiency Certificate (B2) or Medium of Instruction (MOI) declaration
required_documents.notes: B2 certificate (IELTS/TOEFL/CLAT etc.) or proof that English was the medium of instruction; otherwise B2 verified by interview.

required_documents.document_name: Passport (non-EU) / national ID or passport (EU); residence permit (non-EU residents)
required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply in PDF, max 2 MB.

source_quotes.quote: Economics and Data Science, Course Code 11937, Degree Class LM-56 R (Economics), Teaching language English, 120 CFU, 2 years, Genova.
Knowledge of English at least B2 level is also required.

a three-year degree in an economics or business degree class (L-33 economics sciences; L-18 economics and business management sciences) ... or ... a three-year degree in another class and have at least 40 ECTS in [economics SECS-P, statistics SECS-S, mathematics MAT, computer science INF].

Candidates with a graduation mark of less than 99/110 must complete verification ... a written test with open questions ... evaluate the candidate's personal preparation in the fields of economics, mathematics and statistics.
Economics and Data Science: minimum CGPA 75%, admission test/interview: yes.

uncertainty_notes: Programme is open-access; the 'test to be passed' on the catalogue page is the assessment of adequacy of personal preparation, not a TOLC or competitive entrance exam.
Exact dates of the personal-preparation written test/interview are not on the course pages; they are published on the Department of Economics master's admission-test calendar.

EU/spring deadlines reflect the institution-wide UniGeApply window (master's close 28 August 2026) plus the corsi.unige.it enrolment calendar; no programme-specific first-year EU application deadline is separately published.

Non-EU pre-evaluation document list does not explicitly require a Declaration of Value or CIMEA at the application stage; these are typically required at the enrolment/visa stage per central UniGe rules.
